marketState: Neutral overall, with a short-term bullish tilt from hot weather offset by a longer-term storage overhang
summary: NG=F trades at $2.895 front / $2.856 next, essentially at fair value across the curve (spread -$0.03 to -$0.10 out to 14 days), drifting to SLIGHTLY UNDER by +21D as fair value rises toward $3.07. Near-term weather demand is running hot - both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows sit near the top of their 5-year range (rangePos 0.84 / 0.91, +20.9%/+17.3% vs 5y), a genuine short-term bullish catalyst even though the 24h demand reading has pulled back a bit. Seasonality is the more cautious datapoint: price is running 8.1pp below its typical seasonal path, odds of a higher price 3-4 weeks out are sub-50% (38%/38%) with a typical -1.4% 4-week move, but reliability is explicitly Low this week (59pp 10-90% spread) so this signal shouldn't be leaned on heavily; September (historically the strongest month) falls inside the long-term window. Storage remains a structural overhang at +180 Bcf vs the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise build, while COT shows managed money net short but only at the 35th percentile - not extreme or crowded positioning. Technicals show an established bearish trend/EMA structure (50<200) on daily and weekly charts, but RSI in the mid-30s and a positive MACD histogram on 15m/1h/4h hint at a stabilizing setup near current levels.

longTerm.rationale: Signals conflict and the top-priority seasonality read is explicitly Low reliability: price sits 8.1pp below its typical seasonal path with only 38% odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out, yet September (dead ahead in this window) is historically the strongest month. Storage's +180 Bcf surplus and bearish +22 Bcf surprise argue for caps on upside, while fair value drifting from FAIR to SLIGHTLY UNDER by +21D and moderate (not extreme) MM short positioning (35th pct) leave room either way.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback toward the fair-value band low (~$2.50-$2.60) to buy into seasonal September strength with defined risk, or confirmation via a bullish storage print / improving weekly trend (RSI reclaiming 50, weekly EMA50 turning up through EMA200) before committing 3-12 week length. Reassess bearish if the storage surplus widens further and price breaks below $2.70.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (risk ≈ $0.16/MMBtu per contract, size so that equals ~1% of account equity)
shortTerm.rationale: Both weather windows (1-7D and 8-16D) are running hot vs 5-year norms and sit near the top of their range (rangePos 0.84/0.91), a real near-term demand catalyst, while price is still only FAIR vs fair value ($2.895 vs $2.923 fair now) rather than stretched. Daily/weekly technicals remain in a bearish trend structure, but oversold RSI (34.8/36.6) plus positive MACD histogram on 15m/1h/4h suggest a stabilizing bounce is plausible into the hot-weather window.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.72 (support/stop) or a rollover in the 8-16D demand outlook back toward/below 5y normal would invalidate the near-term long thesis.
